document.heading: Friends of Wangari Gardens Grant Emergency Amendment Act of 2026

To amend, on an emergency basis, the Department of Transportation Establishment Act of 2022 to authorize the Director to issue a grant to Friends of Wangari Gardens for the assessment, design, and installation of a water meter and potable water source directly on the Wangari Gardens property with a spigot for gardeners' free use.
document.text: BE IT ENACTED BY THE COUNCIL OF THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, That this act may be cited as the "Friends of Wangari Gardens Grant Emergency Amendment Act of 2026".

section.text: Section 3(c) of the Department of Transportation Establishment Act of 2022, effective May 21, 2002 (D.C. Law 14-137; D.C. Official Code § 50-921.02(c)), is amended by adding a new paragraph (5) to read as follows:

para.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1) of this subsection, the Director may issue a grant to the Friends of Wangari Gardens for the purpose of assessing, designing, and installing a water meter and potable water source directly on the Wangari Gardens property with a spigot for gardeners' free use.

codify:insert.text: *NOTE: This section includes amendments by emergency legislation that will expire on May 31, 2026. To view the text of this section after the expiration of all emergency and temporary legislation, click this link: Past Permanent Version.*

section.heading: Fiscal impact statement.
section.text: The Council adopts the fiscal impact statement of the Budget Director as the fiscal impact statement required by section 4a of the General Legislative Procedures Act of 1975, approved October 16, 2006 (120 Stat. 2038; D.C. Official Code § 1-301.47a).

section.heading: Effective date.
section.text: This act shall take effect following approval by the Mayor (or in the event of veto by the Mayor, action by the Council to override the veto), and shall remain in effect for no longer than 90 days, as provided for emergency acts of the Council of the District of Columbia in section 412(a) of the District of Columbia Home Rule Act, approved December 24, 1973 (87 Stat. 788; D.C. Official Code § 1-204.12(a)).
